The conditions every New England roof survives

This is what your roof fights every single summer.

0in

0mph

Years D.R. Roof has weathered them with you

Sudden downpours overwhelm gutters, wind-driven rain finds every gap in aging flashing, and hail pockmarks shingles that already took a beating over the winter. We spec materials and install methods for New England's summer storm season — not a generic roof that leaks at the first thunderclap.

What we do

One crew. Every roof.

From a single ice-dam leak to a full home exterior overhaul, you deal with the same team start to finish.

01

Residential Roofing

Asphalt, rubber, cedar shake & composite roofing for homes across New England — repairs, re-roofs, and full replacements built to stand up to high winds, driving rain, and hail.

02

Siding & Gutters

Weather-tight siding and properly sized gutter systems that keep heavy summer downpours moving away from your home, not into it.

03

Repairs & Leaks

Storm-torn shingles, wind-lifted flashing, hail damage, and stubborn leaks after a downpour. We find the real source and fix it — not just patch the symptom.

04

Inspections & Estimates

Honest, no-pressure roof inspections with photos and a clear written estimate. You'll know exactly what you need — and what you don't.

How it works

No surprises. No runaround.

01

Free inspection

We climb up, document everything with photos, and tell you the honest condition of your roof.

02

Clear estimate

A written quote with line items and material specs. The number we give you is the number you pay.

03

Clean install

Licensed crew, quality materials, full cleanup. Done on schedule and built to last through New England's storm season.

Trusted on doorsteps across New England.

They are one week in on the project and have gone above and beyond in their communication and consideration for homeowners. Very grateful for their professionalism and customer service.

Bellingham, MA · Condo Complex Roofing
"

They swooped in and saved me from a disaster of failing synthetic underlayment combined with a "what-in-the-world-happened-here" installation job. It has rained many times since this roofing system was installed and I am no longer getting my dehumidifiers, towels, and buckets ready!
